questions.questionText: The integrated management of neonatal and childhood illness (IMNCI) classifies sick children into:
questions.optionB: Pink (urgent referral), yellow (treat at health center), and green (home management) — based on danger signs
questions.explanation: IMNCI (WHO/UNICEF): systematic approach for assessing sick children (2 months to 5 years) and neonates (0-2 months). Color-coded classification: PINK (severe — urgent pre-referral treatment + refer), YELLOW (moderate — specific treatment at health facility), GREEN (mild — home care + counsel). General danger signs: inability to drink/breastfeed, vomiting everything, convulsions, lethargic/unconscious. Assesses: cough/difficulty breathing, diarrhea, fever, ear problems, malnutrition, anemia. Reference: OP Ghai — IMNCI.

questions.questionText: The national immunization schedule in India includes BCG given at:
questions.optionB: Birth (as early as possible, preferably within 24 hours — single dose, intradermal, left upper arm)
questions.explanation: NIS India BCG: at birth (or as soon as possible). Route: intradermal, left upper arm (deltoid insertion). Dose: 0.05 mL (<1 month) or 0.1 mL (>1 month). Leaves a scar (tubercle formation at 3-4 weeks). Birth doses: BCG + OPV-0 + Hepatitis B birth dose. 6 weeks: OPV-1, Pentavalent-1, Rotavirus-1, fIPV-1, PCV-1. 14 weeks: OPV-2, Pentavalent-2, Rotavirus-2. 9 months: MR-1/measles, JE-1, Vitamin A (1st dose). Reference: IAP/NIS — Immunization Schedule.

questions.questionText: The leading cause of under-5 mortality in India is:
questions.optionB: Neonatal causes (prematurity, birth asphyxia, neonatal sepsis — neonatal deaths account for ~55% of under-5 deaths)
questions.explanation: Under-5 mortality in India: neonatal deaths (~55% — first 28 days of life). Leading neonatal causes: prematurity/LBW (#1), birth asphyxia/birth trauma (#2), neonatal sepsis/infections (#3). Post-neonatal (1-59 months): pneumonia (#1 post-neonatal), diarrhea (#2), malaria, measles, malnutrition (underlying in 45% of under-5 deaths). India accounts for highest number of under-5 deaths globally. SDG target: reduce under-5 mortality to <25/1000 live births by 2030. Reference: OP Ghai — Child Mortality.
